input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: The COU's decision regime is a tiny, under-characterized fraction of the training data.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 application-domain coverage; proto-taxonomy D-CCB-15

case_context.cou: surrogate flags the rare fault for a maintenance-safety decision
case_context.decision_consequence: maintenance action on the rare-fault flag

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Overall accuracy is dominated by the 97% nominal class; the COU's rare-fault regime is 0.3% of training with uncharacterized error, so the headline accuracy does not characterize the decision regime.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the fault detector fails (a verdict the coverage gap does not establish)
treats the imbalance-dominated overall accuracy as rare-regime performance

hardness.obvious_signal: overall accuracy is very high (98.5%) on a large, representative-looking dataset
hardness.conflicting_signal: the COU is specifically the rare-fault regime, which is 0.3% of training; the high overall accuracy is dominated by the abundant nominal class, and error in the rare-fault regime the decision depends on is uncharacterized

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 coverage: aggregate accuracy over an imbalanced dataset does not characterize a rare decision regime; the regime needs its own evaluation.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
